generated_oml:
prefix base: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
prefix DTDFVocab: https://example.org/dtdf/vocab#
instance ThermalIncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "A thermal incubator system consisting of a styrofoam insulated container, a heatbed, a fan for air distribution, two internal temperature sensors, one external temperature sensor, and a Raspberry Pi controller. The system operates in a thermal regulation domain, maintaining a set temperature inside the insulated box regardless of external conditions or added content (e.g., cold water). Key characteristics include thermal inertia of the heatbed, uniform temperature distribution (ensured by the fan), and dynamic energy transfer between the air inside the box and the external environment, making it suitable for digital twinning due to its real-time monitoring needs and predictive modeling requirements.",
    base:contains Heatbed, Fan, ExternalTemperatureSensor, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, RaspberryPi4, LowLevelDriver, BangBangController
]
instance Heatbed : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Heatbed for heating in the thermal incubator system"
]
instance Fan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Fan for air circulation in the thermal incubator system"
]
instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor outside for ambient temperature measurement",
    DTDFVocab:producedData AmbientTemperature
]
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "First temperature sensor inside the insulated styrofoam box to monitor internal temperature distribution",
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureRaw1
]
instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second temperature sensor inside the insulated styrofoam box to monitor internal temperature distribution",
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureRaw2
]
instance AmbientTemperature : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor,
    base:desc "Ambient temperature data transmitted via RabbitMQ after reading from external sensor"
]
instance InternalTemperatureRaw1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1,
    base:desc "Raw internal temperature data from first internal sensor"
]
instance InternalTemperatureRaw2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2,
    base:desc "Raw internal temperature data from second internal sensor"
]
instance InternalTemperature : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LowLevelDriver,
    base:desc "Averaged internal temperature from two internal sensors to mitigate thermal non-uniformity"
]
instance AmbientTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Ambient temperature data collected from external sensor",
    DTDFVocab:fromData AmbientTemperature,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine, CalibrationEngine, VisualizationEngine, AnomalyDetectionEngine
]
instance InternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Averaged internal temperature data from two internal sensors",
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperature,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine, CalibrationEngine, VisualizationEngine, AnomalyDetectionEngine
]
instance RaspberryPi4 : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B for hardware control and processing"
]
instance LowLevelDriver : [DTDFVocab:Component, DTDFVocab:Enabler] [
    base:desc "Low-level driver for sensor/actuator interaction and data preprocessing",
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, AnomalyDetectionService
]
instance BangBangController : [DTDFVocab:Component, DTDFVocab:Enabler] [
    base:desc "Bang-bang controller with parameters (LL, UL, H, C) for temperature regulation"
]
instance RabbitMQ : [DTDFVocab:Component, DTDFVocab:Enabler] [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker for communication between components",
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, AnomalyDetectionService, SelfAdaptationService
]
instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model with two free parameters based on all four assumptions (uniform temperature distribution, no heat accumulation in walls, constant specific heat capacity, and complete energy transfer from heatbed to air)",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
]
instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model with four free parameters which relaxes Assumption 4 to account for heatbed thermal inertia",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine, CalibrationEngine
]
instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for thermal simulation services using heat transfer equations",
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]
instance CalibrationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for model calibration services using non-linear least squares solver",
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
]
instance VisualizationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for real-time visualization through time series database and dashboard",
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]
instance AnomalyDetectionEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for anomaly detection and heatbed state estimation using Kalman filter",
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]
instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation services using Model A and Model B to predict internal temperature dynamics",
    DTDFVocab:provides WhatIfSimulations
]
instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Calibration service to adjust model parameters based on experimental data from Physical Twin",
    DTDFVocab:provides RecalibrateModelB
]
instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization through time series database and dashboard by relaying controller and low-level driver messages",
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualization
]
instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection and heatbed state estimation using Kalman filter that correlates measured data with Model B",
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetection, HeatbedStateEstimation
]
instance SelfAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Self-adaptation loop that detects plant behavior changes, schedules experiments, reconfigures controllers, recalibrates Model B, and optimizes controller behavior based on new data",
    DTDFVocab:provides DetectPlantBehaviorChanges, ScheduleExperiments, ReconfigureControllers, OptimizeControllerBehavior
]
instance RealTimeVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization through time series database and dashboard"
]
instance AnomalyDetection : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection performed using Kalman filter"
]
instance HeatbedStateEstimation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Heatbed state estimation for non-measured heatbed temperature using Kalman filter"
]
instance WhatIfSimulations : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "What-if simulations for controller parameter optimization via co-simulations"
]
instance DetectPlantBehaviorChanges : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Detects plant behavior changes",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance ScheduleExperiments : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Schedules experiments",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance ReconfigureControllers : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Reconfigures controllers",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance OptimizeControllerBehavior : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimizes controller behavior based on new data",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance RecalibrateModelB : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recalibrates Model B based on experimental data",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance BangBangControllerParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Bang-bang controller parameters: Lower Limit (LL), Upper Limit (UL), Heating duration (H), Waiting duration (C)",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BangBangController
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands for heatbed and fan are sent via RabbitMQ using a bang-bang controller with parameters LL (lower limit), UL (upper limit), H (heating duration), and C (waiting duration) as defined in Figure 8. The low-level driver validates commands through a safe protocol to ensure safe actuation, with feedback from temperature sensors continuously updating the controller state."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs RabbitMQ as a message broker to facilitate communication between the Physical Twin's Raspberry Pi controller and low-level driver components, where Digital Twin components listen to messages published to RabbitMQ for sensor data and control command synchronization."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model validation is conducted by comparing simulation results with experimental data from section 4, where Model B demonstrated improved accuracy in predicting heatbed thermal inertia. Assumption 1 (uniform temperature distribution) was experimentally tested in section 4. Calibration of models uses a non-linear least squares solver. Uncertainty quantification and error correction mechanisms are not explicitly detailed in the document."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ serves as the central message broker for publish-subscribe communication between physical and digital components. The network architecture routes messages between the Raspberry Pi controller, low-level driver, and DT components, with RabbitMQ deployed either on-premises or in the cloud as a mature, lightweight solution. No bandwidth, latency, or security protocol details are specified."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Edge deployment on Raspberry Pi for physical twin components (controller, low-level driver), with RabbitMQ message broker hosted on-premises or in the cloud. Software is open-source via GitHub, requiring minimal computational resources suitable for edge devices. No specific storage scalability or high-availability mechanisms are documented."
]
